title: Bean Bag Toss Baseball Game
summary: A wall-hung bean bag toss board with a baseball-diamond hole pattern, mounted on a french cleat built from layered 1x4s.
description:
The mounting system doubles as decoration: layer and glue the 1x4 boards together to build a french cleat, then hook that cleat around the 1x2 at the top of the board's back frame so the whole game hangs flush and level without visible hardware on the front. Trim the cleat's ends to fit your specific wall spot before hanging.

Mark out the baseball-diamond hole pattern on the plywood face after it's attached to the frame, then drill each hole with a 4" hole saw and sand the edges smooth — bean bags need a clean, splinter-free opening to drop through reliably.

steps.title: Build the basic frame
steps.body: Build the basic frame of the project using 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the back support
steps.body: Attach the back support to the top of the frame with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the front bottom
steps.body: Attach the front bottom piece with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the playing surface and drill the holes
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" plywood to the front of the frame with 3/4" brad nails and glue. Mark out the baseball-diamond hole pattern and drill the holes with a 4" hole saw. Sand any rough edges smooth.

steps.title: Build the french cleat and hang
steps.body: Layer the 1x4s over each other and nail and glue together to build a french cleat. Make sure the cleat fits inside the back of the project and hooks around the 1x2 at the top. Trim the ends if necessary, then hang the cleat on the wall in the desired location and hang the game on it.

images.alt: A wall-hung bean bag toss board with a baseball diamond hole pattern
